mern.title: Web Fundamentals
mern.description: The foundation of everything web. Learn how browsers render pages, how to structure content with HTML, style it with CSS, and add interactivity with JavaScript.
mern.challenge: Build a fully responsive personal portfolio page using only HTML & CSS.

mern.title: JavaScript Deep Dive
mern.description: Go beyond the basics. Master modern JavaScript features, asynchronous patterns, and how to communicate with external APIs.
topics: ES6+ — destructuring, spread, arrow functions, modules
mern.challenge: Build a weather app that fetches live data from a public API and handles loading/error states.

mern.title: React Fundamentals
mern.description: Learn how to think in components. Build dynamic UIs with React, understand the component lifecycle, and manage local state.
mern.challenge: Build a task manager app with add, delete, and filter functionality — no backend yet.

mern.title: React Advanced
mern.description: Level up your React skills with global state management, client-side routing, and reusable hook patterns.
topics: React Router v6 — nested routes, params
mern.challenge: Extend your task manager with multi-page routing, a global theme toggle, and persisted state.

mern.title: Node.js & Express
mern.description: Move to the server. Build REST APIs with Node.js and Express, understand middleware, and handle routing and error management.
mern.challenge: Build a REST API for a blog — CRUD endpoints for posts, with input validation and error handling.

mern.title: MongoDB & Mongoose
mern.description: Persist your data. Learn NoSQL database design, connect MongoDB to your Express app, and model data with Mongoose schemas.
topics: NoSQL vs SQL — when to use MongoDB
CRUD operations with the MongoDB driver

resources.label: Web Dev Simplified — MongoDB Crash Course

mern.challenge: Add a MongoDB database to your blog API — store posts and authors with a proper schema and relationships.

mern.title: Authentication & Security
mern.description: Secure your app. Implement user registration and login, hash passwords, issue JWTs, and protect routes.
mern.challenge: Add full auth to your blog API — register, login, JWT issuance, and protected post creation routes.

mern.title: Full Stack Integration
mern.description: Connect your React frontend to your Express backend. Handle API calls, loading states, caching, and global state across the full stack.
topics: Handling auth tokens on the client
Proxy config for local dev (Vite / CRA)
mern.challenge: Build a full MERN blog — React frontend that talks to your Express/MongoDB backend with auth, protected routes, and optimistic UI.

mern.title: Testing
mern.description: Write tests that give you confidence. Cover your React components, API endpoints, and async logic.
topics: React Testing Library — render, queries, events
mern.challenge: Write unit + integration tests for your blog app — cover components, API routes, and auth flow.

mern.title: Deployment & DevOps
mern.description: Ship your app to the world. Deploy your frontend and backend, automate with CI/CD, and containerize with Docker.
topics: Deploying Express API to Railway / Render
GitHub Actions — CI pipeline on push

resources.label: TechWorld with Nana — Docker for Beginners

mern.challenge: Deploy your full MERN blog app — frontend on Vercel, API on Railway, MongoDB on Atlas, with a GitHub Actions CI pipeline.

pern.title: Web Fundamentals
pern.description: Every PERN app ships HTML, CSS and JavaScript to the browser. Learn how pages are structured, styled and made interactive before touching any framework.
pern.challenge: Build a responsive landing page with a working contact form using only HTML and CSS.

pern.title: Modern JavaScript & TypeScript
pern.description: Write the kind of JavaScript a PERN codebase actually uses, then add static types with TypeScript to catch bugs before runtime.
topics: Array & object methods — map, filter, reduce
pern.challenge: Convert a small JavaScript utility library to TypeScript with full type coverage and no 'any'.

pern.title: React Fundamentals
pern.description: Build the 'R' in PERN. Think in components, manage local state, and render dynamic UIs with React and modern hooks.
pern.challenge: Build a task board that fetches, adds, filters and deletes tasks against a mock API.

pern.title: React Advanced & State
pern.description: Scale your React app with routing, global state and server-state caching — the patterns real production frontends rely on.
pern.challenge: Add multi-page routing, a global theme store, and cached server data to your task board.

pern.title: Node.js & Express APIs
pern.description: Build the 'E' and 'N' in PERN. Create RESTful APIs with Express, structure controllers and middleware, and handle errors cleanly.
pern.challenge: Build a REST API for a notes app with CRUD routes, validation, and centralized error handling.

pern.title: Relational Databases & SQL
pern.description: PERN is relational at its core. Learn to model data into tables and query it fluently with SQL before wiring it to Node.
pern.challenge: Design a normalized schema for a blog (users, posts, comments, tags) and write the queries to power its pages.

pern.title: PostgreSQL in Depth
pern.description: Go beyond basic SQL into the features that make PostgreSQL powerful — indexing, transactions, JSON and performance.
pern.challenge: Add indexes and use EXPLAIN ANALYZE to cut a slow blog query's execution time, then store post metadata in JSONB.

pern.title: Data Access Layer
pern.description: Connect Express to PostgreSQL through a typed data layer. Run migrations, model relations, and avoid SQL injection.
pern.challenge: Replace raw SQL in your notes API with Prisma, add a migration, and wrap a multi-step write in a transaction.

pern.title: Auth, Security & Validation
pern.description: Secure the full stack. Implement registration and login, protect routes, validate input, and follow OWASP basics.
pern.challenge: Add register/login with hashed passwords and JWTs, validate every request body with Zod, and protect write routes.

pern.title: Testing, CI/CD & Deployment
pern.description: Ship your PERN app with confidence. Test the stack, containerize it, automate CI, and deploy frontend, API and database.
topics: Deploying React to Vercel & API to Railway/Render
Managed Postgres & connection strings in prod
pern.challenge: Containerize the full app with Docker Compose, add a CI pipeline that runs tests, and deploy it end to end.

t3.title: TypeScript and Modern JavaScript Foundations
t3.description: This layer builds the type-safe JavaScript foundation the entire T3 stack relies on. Strong typing skills make every later tool in the stack easier to use and far less error prone.
topics: Static types, interfaces, and type aliases
t3.challenge: Build a typed command-line task tracker that stores tasks in a JSON file.

t3.title: React Fundamentals
t3.description: This layer covers building component-based user interfaces with React and TypeScript. Mastering components, props, and state is essential before adding the rest of the stack.
t3.challenge: Convert the task tracker into a React single-page app with add and complete actions.

t3.title: Next.js App Router
t3.description: This layer introduces Next.js and its App Router for routing, layouts, and rendering strategies. It is the framework backbone that ties the T3 client and server together.
t3.challenge: Scaffold the task tracker as a Next.js app with separate list and detail routes.

t3.title: Styling with Tailwind CSS
t3.description: This layer teaches utility-first styling to build responsive interfaces quickly. Tailwind keeps styles consistent and co-located with components across the project.
topics: Utility classes and the box model
t3.challenge: Restyle the task tracker with Tailwind into a polished, fully responsive layout.

t3.title: End-to-End Type Safety with tRPC
t3.description: This layer connects client and server with fully typed API calls and no schemas to hand-write. tRPC removes a whole class of bugs by sharing types across the boundary.
t3.challenge: Replace the task tracker fetch calls with typed tRPC queries and mutations.

t3.title: Database Modeling with Prisma
t3.description: This layer covers defining your data model and querying it with a type-safe ORM. Prisma turns your schema into typed client code that pairs naturally with tRPC.
t3.challenge: Persist tasks in PostgreSQL through Prisma models wired into your tRPC routers.

t3.title: Authentication and Sessions
t3.description: This layer adds user accounts, login providers, and protected routes. Authentication is required before the app can store data that belongs to specific users.
t3.challenge: Add GitHub login so each user only sees and edits their own tasks.

t3.title: Advanced State and Data Fetching
t3.description: This layer covers caching, mutations, and optimistic updates for a snappy user experience. Understanding the React Query cache that powers tRPC unlocks reliable real-time-feeling apps.
t3.challenge: Add optimistic create and delete with automatic cache invalidation to the task tracker.

t3.title: Forms, Validation, and Reusable UI
t3.description: This layer builds robust forms with shared validation logic across client and server. Consistent validation and accessible components raise the quality of the whole product.
t3.challenge: Build a validated task editor form that reuses one Zod schema on client and server.

t3.title: Testing and Deployment
t3.description: This layer covers automated testing and shipping the app to production. Reliable tests and a smooth deploy pipeline turn your project into a real maintainable product.
t3.challenge: Add a test suite and deploy the finished task tracker to Vercel with a hosted database.

fullstack-engineer.title: Web Fundamentals
fullstack-engineer.description: This layer establishes the core building blocks of every web page and app. Solid fundamentals in markup, styling, and scripting are prerequisites for all full stack work.
topics: CSS layout with flexbox and grid
fullstack-engineer.challenge: Build a static personal portfolio page with responsive layout and a contact section.

fullstack-engineer.title: Frontend with React
fullstack-engineer.description: This layer teaches building dynamic interfaces with React components and hooks. React is the foundation for the interactive client of the full stack application.
fullstack-engineer.challenge: Build the frontend of a recipe app that lists and filters recipes from a mock API.

fullstack-engineer.title: Backend with Node and Express
fullstack-engineer.description: This layer covers building HTTP servers and REST endpoints with Node and Express. The backend is where business logic and data access live for the application.
fullstack-engineer.challenge: Create an Express API that serves recipes and supports create and read endpoints.

fullstack-engineer.title: Relational Databases and SQL
fullstack-engineer.description: This layer teaches modeling and querying data in a relational database. Persistent storage with SQL is the backbone of most production applications.
fullstack-engineer.challenge: Move recipe storage into PostgreSQL with tables for recipes and ingredients.

fullstack-engineer.title: Connecting Frontend and Backend
fullstack-engineer.description: This layer wires the React client to the Express API over HTTP. Understanding the full request lifecycle is what makes you a true full stack engineer.
fullstack-engineer.challenge: Connect the React recipe app to your live API for full create and list flows.

fullstack-engineer.title: Authentication and Authorization
fullstack-engineer.description: This layer adds user accounts, secure login, and protected routes. Authentication is essential before users can own and manage their own data.
fullstack-engineer.challenge: Add signup and login so users can save private recipe collections.

fullstack-engineer.title: API Design and Advanced Backend
fullstack-engineer.description: This layer deepens backend skills with validation, layered architecture, and performance. Well-designed APIs scale better and are far easier to maintain.
fullstack-engineer.challenge: Refactor the recipe API into layered services with validation and rate limiting.

fullstack-engineer.title: Containers with Docker
fullstack-engineer.description: This layer teaches packaging the app and its database into reproducible containers. Docker removes works-on-my-machine problems and prepares the app for deployment.
fullstack-engineer.challenge: Containerize the API, frontend, and database and run them with Docker Compose.

fullstack-engineer.title: Testing the Full Stack
fullstack-engineer.description: This layer covers automated testing across the backend and frontend. A reliable test suite catches regressions and gives confidence to keep shipping.
fullstack-engineer.challenge: Write unit, API, and end-to-end tests covering the recipe app core flows.

fullstack-engineer.title: Deployment and CI/CD
fullstack-engineer.description: This layer covers shipping the containerized app to the cloud with an automated pipeline. Continuous delivery is what turns a project into a maintained production service.
fullstack-engineer.challenge: Set up a CI/CD pipeline that builds, tests, and deploys the recipe app to the cloud.

mean.title: JavaScript and Web Fundamentals
mean.description: This layer grounds you in the JavaScript and web basics that the whole MEAN stack shares. A strong foundation makes both the Angular frontend and Node backend approachable.
mean.challenge: Build an interactive checklist page using plain JavaScript and the DOM.

mean.title: TypeScript for Angular
mean.description: This layer introduces TypeScript, which Angular uses throughout. Understanding types and decorators prepares you for Angular's structured, class-based approach.
mean.challenge: Rewrite the checklist logic as typed TypeScript classes and interfaces.

mean.title: Angular Fundamentals
mean.description: This layer covers building component-driven applications with the Angular framework. Angular provides the structured frontend for the MEAN stack.
mean.challenge: Build an Angular task board with components, routing, and a task service.

mean.title: Reactive Programming with RxJS
mean.description: This layer teaches observables and reactive data flow that power Angular's HTTP and forms. RxJS lets you handle async streams cleanly across the app.
topics: Common operators like map and filter
mean.challenge: Add live search filtering to the task board using RxJS observables.

mean.title: Node and Express Backend
mean.description: This layer covers building the API server with Node and Express. Express is the application framework that serves data to the Angular frontend.
mean.challenge: Build an Express API exposing CRUD routes for the task board.

mean.title: MongoDB and Mongoose
mean.description: This layer teaches storing and querying data in MongoDB through Mongoose models. The document database completes the data layer of the MEAN stack.
mean.challenge: Persist tasks in MongoDB using Mongoose models wired into your Express routes.

mean.title: Connecting Angular to the API
mean.description: This layer wires the Angular client to the Express and MongoDB backend. Connecting the layers makes the application a working end-to-end product.
mean.challenge: Connect the Angular task board to your live API for full CRUD over HTTP.

mean.title: Authentication with JWT
mean.description: This layer secures the stack with token-based login and protected routes. Authentication lets users manage their own private task data.
mean.challenge: Add JWT login with Angular route guards so users see only their own tasks.

mean.title: Advanced Angular and Forms
mean.description: This layer covers reactive forms, state management, and performance patterns. These skills make complex Angular apps maintainable and responsive.
mean.challenge: Build a validated reactive form for creating and editing tasks with subtasks.

mean.title: Testing and Deployment
mean.description: This layer covers testing the app and deploying it to production. Tests and a hosted environment turn the project into a real, reliable product.
mean.challenge: Add tests and deploy the full MEAN task board with a hosted Atlas database.

jamstack.title: Web Foundations and the JAMstack Model
jamstack.description: This layer covers web basics and the core idea of pre-rendered markup with APIs. Understanding the JAMstack philosophy frames every tool you learn later.
jamstack.challenge: Build a static landing page that pulls a quote from a public API.

jamstack.title: React and Component Thinking
jamstack.description: This layer teaches building reusable UI with React, the basis for both Next.js and Gatsby. Component thinking is central to modern JAMstack frameworks.
jamstack.challenge: Rebuild the landing page as reusable React components with a quote card.

jamstack.title: Static Sites with Next.js
jamstack.description: This layer covers static and hybrid rendering with Next.js. Next.js is a leading framework for fast, SEO-friendly JAMstack sites.
jamstack.challenge: Create a statically generated blog index with a Next.js page per post.

jamstack.title: Gatsby and the Data Layer
jamstack.description: This layer introduces Gatsby and its GraphQL data layer for sourcing content. Gatsby excels at building content-rich, image-heavy static sites.
jamstack.challenge: Build a Gatsby photo gallery sourcing images and metadata through GraphQL.

jamstack.title: Headless CMS with Contentful
jamstack.description: This layer teaches managing content in a headless CMS and pulling it into your site. Decoupling content from code lets non-developers update the site safely.
jamstack.challenge: Move the blog content into Contentful and render posts from the CMS.

jamstack.title: APIs and Serverless Functions
jamstack.description: This layer adds dynamic behavior through serverless functions and APIs. Serverless logic provides the A in JAMstack without managing servers.
jamstack.challenge: Add a serverless contact form and a newsletter signup function to the blog.

jamstack.title: Authentication and Identity
jamstack.description: This layer adds user accounts and gated content to an otherwise static site. Identity lets you offer members-only areas without a traditional backend.
jamstack.challenge: Add member login that unlocks a subscriber-only section of the blog.

jamstack.title: Performance and SEO
jamstack.description: This layer focuses on making JAMstack sites fast and discoverable. Performance and SEO are core selling points of the JAMstack approach.
jamstack.challenge: Audit and optimize the blog to reach strong Lighthouse scores across the board.

jamstack.title: E-commerce and Dynamic Data
jamstack.description: This layer adds commerce and richer dynamic data to a static foundation. It shows how JAMstack scales from blogs to real transactional sites.
jamstack.challenge: Add a small store to the site with CMS products and Stripe checkout.

jamstack.title: Deployment and Continuous Delivery
jamstack.description: This layer covers deploying and automating builds for JAMstack sites. Git-based continuous delivery is what makes JAMstack workflows so smooth in production.
jamstack.challenge: Deploy the full site to Netlify with deploy previews and CMS-triggered builds.

django-react.title: Python Fundamentals
django-react.description: This layer establishes core Python skills that Django is built on. A firm grasp of the language makes the framework far easier to learn.
django-react.challenge: Write a Python script that manages a simple address book in memory.

django-react.title: Django Basics
django-react.description: This layer introduces the Django framework, its project structure, and the request cycle. Django provides the batteries-included backend for the stack.
django-react.challenge: Build a Django app that lists blog posts rendered from server-side views.

django-react.title: Django Models and the ORM
django-react.description: This layer covers modeling data and querying it with the Django ORM. The ORM is how Django apps store and retrieve persistent data.
django-react.challenge: Model posts and comments in PostgreSQL and query them through the ORM.

django-react.title: Building APIs with Django REST Framework
django-react.description: This layer teaches exposing your data as a REST API with DRF. The API is the bridge that lets a React frontend talk to Django.
django-react.challenge: Expose CRUD API endpoints for posts and comments using DRF viewsets.

django-react.title: React Frontend
django-react.description: This layer covers building the React client that consumes the Django API. React provides the interactive frontend half of the stack.
django-react.challenge: Build a React frontend that lists posts fetched from the Django API.

django-react.title: Connecting React and Django
django-react.description: This layer wires the React client and Django API together over HTTP. Integrating the two halves produces a working full stack application.
django-react.challenge: Wire the React app to create, edit, and delete posts through the Django API.

django-react.title: Authentication with Tokens
django-react.description: This layer adds secure login and protected endpoints across the stack. Authentication lets users own and manage their own content.
django-react.challenge: Add JWT login so users can manage only their own posts on both sides.

django-react.title: Advanced Django
django-react.description: This layer covers performance, background tasks, and advanced backend features. These skills prepare a Django app to handle real production workloads.
django-react.challenge: Add image uploads and a background email task when a new post is published.

django-react.title: Advanced React State and UX
django-react.description: This layer improves the frontend with caching, data fetching libraries, and polished UX. Mature client patterns make the app feel fast and reliable.
django-react.challenge: Add React Query caching and optimistic post updates to the frontend.

django-react.title: Testing and Deployment
django-react.description: This layer covers testing the stack and deploying it to production. Tests and a real deployment pipeline turn the project into a maintainable product.
django-react.challenge: Add tests, containerize the app, and deploy the Django and React blog to the cloud.

rails-dev.title: Ruby Fundamentals
rails-dev.description: This layer builds core Ruby skills that the Rails framework depends on. Comfort with Ruby idioms makes Rails feel natural and productive.
rails-dev.challenge: Write a Ruby program that manages a small library catalog using classes.

rails-dev.title: Rails Basics and MVC
rails-dev.description: This layer introduces Rails conventions, the MVC pattern, and the request cycle. Understanding Rails structure is the foundation for everything else.
rails-dev.challenge: Generate a Rails app that lists and shows articles using controllers and views.

rails-dev.title: Active Record and Databases
rails-dev.description: This layer teaches modeling data and querying it with Active Record. The ORM is how Rails apps persist and relate their data.
rails-dev.challenge: Model articles and comments in PostgreSQL with validations and associations.

rails-dev.title: Views, Forms, and Assets
rails-dev.description: This layer covers building the server-rendered frontend with views, forms, and the asset pipeline. Polished views turn a working backend into a usable product.
rails-dev.challenge: Add forms to create and edit articles with validation feedback and styled layouts.

rails-dev.title: Authentication and Authorization
rails-dev.description: This layer adds user accounts and access control to the application. Authentication lets users own and manage their own content securely.
topics: User signup and login with Devise
rails-dev.challenge: Add login so users can only edit and delete the articles they authored.

rails-dev.title: Interactivity with Hotwire
rails-dev.description: This layer brings modern interactivity to Rails without a heavy JavaScript framework. Hotwire is the Rails-native way to build fast, reactive interfaces.
rails-dev.challenge: Use Turbo and Stimulus to add inline comment posting without full page reloads.

rails-dev.title: Background Jobs and Mailers
rails-dev.description: This layer covers running work in the background and sending email. Offloading slow tasks keeps the app responsive and reliable.
rails-dev.challenge: Send a welcome email through a background job when a user signs up.

rails-dev.title: Building APIs with Rails
rails-dev.description: This layer covers exposing your app as a JSON API for clients and integrations. API mode broadens what your Rails backend can power.
rails-dev.challenge: Add a versioned JSON API exposing articles for an external client.

rails-dev.title: Performance and Advanced Patterns
rails-dev.description: This layer covers optimizing queries, caching, and structuring larger Rails apps. These practices keep a growing codebase fast and maintainable.
topics: N plus one queries and eager loading
rails-dev.challenge: Profile the app, fix N plus one queries, and add caching to the article list.

rails-dev.title: Testing and Deployment
rails-dev.description: This layer covers automated testing and deploying Rails to production. A solid test suite and deploy pipeline make the app reliable and maintainable.
topics: Model and request specs with RSpec
rails-dev.challenge: Add an RSpec suite and deploy the finished Rails blog to a cloud platform.

next-fullstack.title: TypeScript and React Foundations
next-fullstack.description: This layer grounds you in TypeScript and React, the core of Next.js development. These skills underpin every feature you build in the framework.
next-fullstack.challenge: Build a typed React component library with a button, card, and input.

next-fullstack.title: Next.js App Router and Routing
next-fullstack.description: This layer covers routing, layouts, and navigation with the Next.js App Router. The router defines how pages and shared UI are structured.
next-fullstack.challenge: Scaffold a dashboard app with nested layouts and dynamic project routes.

next-fullstack.title: Server Components and Rendering
next-fullstack.description: This layer explains server and client components and Next.js rendering strategies. Choosing the right rendering model is key to performance and correctness.
next-fullstack.challenge: Render the dashboard data on the server with streaming and Suspense boundaries.

next-fullstack.title: Database with Prisma
next-fullstack.description: This layer covers modeling and querying data with Prisma. The database layer gives your Next.js app persistent, typed data.
next-fullstack.challenge: Model projects and tasks in PostgreSQL and query them from server components.

next-fullstack.title: Server Actions and Mutations
next-fullstack.description: This layer teaches mutating data with server actions and route handlers. Server actions let you write secure backend logic right alongside your UI.
next-fullstack.challenge: Add create, edit, and delete tasks using server actions with Zod validation.

next-fullstack.title: Authentication
next-fullstack.description: This layer adds login, sessions, and protected routes to the app. Authentication enables per-user data and secure access control.
topics: Reading the session on the server
next-fullstack.challenge: Add authentication so each user sees and manages only their own projects.

next-fullstack.title: Styling and UI
next-fullstack.description: This layer covers building an accessible, responsive interface with Tailwind and component libraries. A clean UI makes the full stack app feel professional.
next-fullstack.challenge: Build a polished, responsive dashboard UI with reusable styled components.

next-fullstack.title: Advanced Data and Caching
next-fullstack.description: This layer covers Next.js caching layers, revalidation, and optimistic UI. Mastering caching is what makes Next.js apps both fast and correct.
next-fullstack.challenge: Add tag-based revalidation and optimistic task updates to the dashboard.

next-fullstack.title: API Routes and Integrations
next-fullstack.description: This layer covers building APIs and integrating third-party services. Real apps depend on external services for payments, email, and more.
next-fullstack.challenge: Add a paid pro plan with Stripe checkout and a webhook that updates the user.

next-fullstack.title: Testing and Deployment to Vercel
next-fullstack.description: This layer covers testing the app and deploying it to production on Vercel. Tests and a smooth deploy pipeline make the project a real product.
next-fullstack.challenge: Add tests and deploy the dashboard to Vercel with a hosted database and previews.

sveltekit-dev.title: JavaScript and TypeScript Foundations
sveltekit-dev.description: This layer grounds you in the language fundamentals Svelte builds on. Comfort with modern JavaScript and types makes Svelte development smooth.
sveltekit-dev.challenge: Build a small typed utility module with functions to format dates and currency.

sveltekit-dev.title: Svelte Fundamentals
sveltekit-dev.description: This layer covers building components with Svelte's reactive, compiler-based model. Svelte is the foundation that SvelteKit extends into a full framework.
sveltekit-dev.challenge: Build a reactive counter and a reusable card component in Svelte.

sveltekit-dev.title: SvelteKit Routing and Pages
sveltekit-dev.description: This layer introduces SvelteKit's file-based routing, layouts, and project structure. Routing is the backbone of building full applications with SvelteKit.
sveltekit-dev.challenge: Scaffold a notes app with a list page, dynamic note route, and shared layout.

sveltekit-dev.title: Loading Data
sveltekit-dev.description: This layer covers loading data on the server and client with load functions. Proper data loading is essential for fast, SEO-friendly pages.
sveltekit-dev.challenge: Load notes from an external API using server load functions with typed data.

sveltekit-dev.title: Form Actions and Mutations
sveltekit-dev.description: This layer teaches handling form submissions and mutations with form actions. Form actions provide progressive, secure server-side data changes.
sveltekit-dev.challenge: Add create, edit, and delete notes using form actions with Zod validation.

sveltekit-dev.title: Databases and Persistence
sveltekit-dev.description: This layer covers persisting data with a database and ORM in SvelteKit. Real storage turns the notes demo into a durable application.
sveltekit-dev.challenge: Persist notes in PostgreSQL via Prisma inside your server load and actions.

sveltekit-dev.title: Authentication and Hooks
sveltekit-dev.description: This layer adds sessions, login, and request hooks for protected routes. Authentication enables per-user data and secure access control.
sveltekit-dev.challenge: Add login so each user can manage only their own notes using hooks and sessions.

sveltekit-dev.title: Advanced UI and Stores
sveltekit-dev.description: This layer covers shared state, animations, and polished interactions. Rich UI patterns make the application feel smooth and professional.
sveltekit-dev.challenge: Add a theme store and animated transitions when notes are added or removed.

sveltekit-dev.title: APIs and Advanced SvelteKit
sveltekit-dev.description: This layer covers building API endpoints and advanced rendering options. These features let SvelteKit power APIs and integrate with other clients.
sveltekit-dev.challenge: Add a JSON API endpoint that exposes notes for an external client.

sveltekit-dev.title: Testing and Deployment
sveltekit-dev.description: This layer covers testing the app and deploying it to production. Tests and an adapter-based deploy make the project a real, reliable product.
sveltekit-dev.challenge: Add tests and deploy the SvelteKit notes app with a hosted database.

nuxt-dev.title: JavaScript and TypeScript Foundations
nuxt-dev.description: This layer grounds you in the language fundamentals Vue and Nuxt build on. Solid JavaScript and types make the rest of the stack approachable.
nuxt-dev.challenge: Build a typed module of helper functions for formatting and filtering data.

nuxt-dev.title: Vue 3 Fundamentals
nuxt-dev.description: This layer covers building reactive components with Vue 3 and the Composition API. Vue is the framework that Nuxt extends into a full stack tool.
nuxt-dev.challenge: Build a reactive product list component with search and a reusable card.

nuxt-dev.title: Nuxt Basics and Routing
nuxt-dev.description: This layer introduces Nuxt structure, file-based routing, and layouts. Nuxt conventions provide the scaffolding for full applications.
nuxt-dev.challenge: Scaffold a shop app with a products page, dynamic product route, and layout.

nuxt-dev.title: Data Fetching and Rendering
nuxt-dev.description: This layer covers fetching data and Nuxt's rendering modes. Choosing the right rendering and fetch strategy is key to performance and SEO.
nuxt-dev.challenge: Fetch products from an external API with useFetch and add page metadata.

nuxt-dev.title: Server Engine with Nitro
nuxt-dev.description: This layer covers building backend API routes with the Nitro server engine. Nitro lets Nuxt apps include their own server logic and endpoints.
nuxt-dev.challenge: Add Nitro API routes that serve and create products for the shop app.

nuxt-dev.title: Databases and Persistence
nuxt-dev.description: This layer covers persisting data through a database in your Nitro server routes. Real storage turns the demo into a durable application.
nuxt-dev.challenge: Persist products in PostgreSQL via Prisma inside your Nitro API routes.

nuxt-dev.title: State and Authentication
nuxt-dev.description: This layer covers shared state with Pinia and adding user authentication. State and auth enable carts, sessions, and per-user data.
topics: Accessing the user on server and client
nuxt-dev.challenge: Add login and a Pinia-backed cart so users manage their own orders.

nuxt-dev.title: Modules and Advanced Patterns
nuxt-dev.description: This layer covers the Nuxt module ecosystem and reusable patterns. Modules and composables speed up development and keep code clean.
nuxt-dev.challenge: Add image optimization and a custom composable for currency formatting.

nuxt-dev.title: Performance and SEO
nuxt-dev.description: This layer focuses on making Nuxt apps fast and discoverable. Performance and SEO are central strengths of server-rendered Nuxt apps.
nuxt-dev.challenge: Audit and optimize the shop for strong Lighthouse and SEO scores.

nuxt-dev.title: Testing and Deployment
nuxt-dev.description: This layer covers testing the app and deploying it to production. Tests and a smooth deploy turn the project into a real, reliable product.
nuxt-dev.challenge: Add tests and deploy the Nuxt shop app with a hosted database.

astro-dev.title: Web and TypeScript Foundations
astro-dev.description: This layer covers the web fundamentals and types that Astro builds on. A strong base in markup, styling, and TypeScript makes Astro intuitive.
astro-dev.challenge: Build a static styled landing page with semantic, responsive HTML and CSS.

astro-dev.title: Astro Basics and Components
astro-dev.description: This layer introduces Astro components, the .astro syntax, and the project structure. Understanding Astro's component model is the entry point to the framework.
astro-dev.challenge: Rebuild the landing page as reusable Astro components with props and slots.

astro-dev.title: Routing, Layouts, and Pages
astro-dev.description: This layer covers file-based routing, layouts, and dynamic pages in Astro. Routing and layouts are how you assemble a multi-page site.
astro-dev.challenge: Create a multi-page blog with a shared layout and dynamic post routes.

astro-dev.title: Content Collections and MDX
astro-dev.description: This layer covers managing content with collections, Markdown, and MDX. Content collections give Astro a type-safe content layer for blogs and docs.
astro-dev.challenge: Move blog posts into a typed content collection authored in MDX.

astro-dev.title: Islands and Interactivity
astro-dev.description: This layer teaches Astro's islands architecture for adding interactive components. Islands let you ship mostly static HTML with targeted client-side interactivity.
astro-dev.challenge: Add an interactive search island to the blog using a framework component.

astro-dev.title: Styling and Design Systems
astro-dev.description: This layer covers building a cohesive, responsive design with Tailwind in Astro. Consistent styling makes a content site look polished and trustworthy.
astro-dev.challenge: Apply a Tailwind design system with dark mode across the blog and posts.

astro-dev.title: Server Rendering and Endpoints
astro-dev.description: This layer covers server-side rendering, adapters, and API endpoints. On-demand rendering lets Astro handle dynamic data and form submissions.
astro-dev.challenge: Add a server-rendered contact form backed by an Astro API endpoint.

astro-dev.title: Data, CMS, and Integrations
astro-dev.description: This layer covers pulling data from APIs and a headless CMS into Astro. External content sources let non-developers manage the site safely.
astro-dev.challenge: Source blog posts from a headless CMS with optimized cover images.

astro-dev.title: Performance and SEO
astro-dev.description: This layer focuses on Astro's performance strengths and search optimization. Fast, well-indexed sites are the core promise of the Astro framework.
astro-dev.challenge: Add an RSS feed and sitemap and tune the blog to top Lighthouse scores.

astro-dev.title: Testing and Deployment
astro-dev.description: This layer covers testing the site and deploying it to production. Tests and an adapter-based deploy make the project a real, reliable product.
astro-dev.challenge: Add tests and deploy the finished Astro blog with preview builds enabled.

laravel-vue.title: PHP Fundamentals
laravel-vue.description: This layer establishes modern PHP skills that Laravel is built on. A solid language base makes the framework far easier to adopt.
laravel-vue.challenge: Write a PHP script that processes a CSV of products into formatted output.

laravel-vue.title: Laravel Basics and Routing
laravel-vue.description: This layer introduces Laravel structure, routing, controllers, and views. Understanding the request lifecycle is the foundation for the backend.
laravel-vue.challenge: Build a Laravel app that lists products through routes, controllers, and Blade.

laravel-vue.title: Eloquent and Databases
laravel-vue.description: This layer covers modeling and querying data with the Eloquent ORM. Eloquent and migrations give the app reliable persistent storage.
laravel-vue.challenge: Model products and categories in MySQL with Eloquent relationships and seeders.

laravel-vue.title: Validation, Forms, and Auth
laravel-vue.description: This layer covers request validation, forms, and built-in authentication. Secure forms and login are essential to a usable application.
laravel-vue.challenge: Add product create and edit forms with validation and login-gated access.

laravel-vue.title: Vue 3 Fundamentals
laravel-vue.description: This layer introduces building reactive interfaces with Vue 3 and the Composition API. Vue provides the dynamic frontend half of the stack.
laravel-vue.challenge: Build a Vue product filter component with reactive search and sorting.

laravel-vue.title: Building APIs with Laravel
laravel-vue.description: This layer covers exposing Laravel as a JSON API for the Vue frontend. A clean API decouples the backend from the client.
laravel-vue.challenge: Build a JSON API for products with Sanctum token authentication.

laravel-vue.title: Connecting Vue to Laravel
laravel-vue.description: This layer wires the Vue frontend to the Laravel API and manages client state. Integration produces a complete single-page application.
laravel-vue.challenge: Build a Vue SPA that lists and manages products through the Laravel API.

laravel-vue.title: Queues, Jobs, and Email
laravel-vue.description: This layer covers background processing and sending email in Laravel. Offloading slow work keeps the app fast and responsive.
laravel-vue.challenge: Send an order confirmation email through a queued job when checkout completes.

laravel-vue.title: Advanced Patterns and Performance
laravel-vue.description: This layer covers caching, optimization, and clean architecture in larger apps. These practices keep a growing Laravel codebase fast and maintainable.
topics: Eager loading and N plus one
laravel-vue.challenge: Profile and optimize the product API with eager loading and Redis caching.

laravel-vue.title: Testing and Deployment
laravel-vue.description: This layer covers testing the stack and deploying it to production. Tests and a deploy pipeline turn the project into a maintainable product.
topics: Feature and unit tests with Pest
laravel-vue.challenge: Add tests and deploy the Laravel and Vue store to a cloud platform.

remix-dev.title: TypeScript and React Foundations
remix-dev.description: This layer grounds you in TypeScript and React, the core of Remix development. These skills underpin every route and component you build.
remix-dev.challenge: Build a small typed React component set with a list, item, and form.

remix-dev.title: Remix Basics and Routing
remix-dev.description: This layer introduces Remix structure, nested routing, and the web fundamentals it embraces. Routing is the backbone of every Remix application.
remix-dev.challenge: Scaffold a contacts app with nested routes and a shared sidebar layout.

remix-dev.title: Loaders and Data Loading
remix-dev.description: This layer covers loading data on the server with loaders. Loaders are how Remix fetches data before rendering each route.
remix-dev.challenge: Load contacts from a data source using loaders with typed loader data.

remix-dev.title: Actions, Forms, and Mutations
remix-dev.description: This layer covers handling form submissions and mutations with actions. Remix uses standard forms and actions for progressive, resilient data changes.
remix-dev.challenge: Add create, edit, and delete contacts using actions with Zod validation.

remix-dev.title: Databases and Persistence
remix-dev.description: This layer covers persisting data through a database and ORM in Remix. Real storage turns the contacts demo into a durable application.
remix-dev.challenge: Persist contacts in PostgreSQL via Prisma inside your loaders and actions.

remix-dev.title: Authentication and Sessions
remix-dev.description: This layer adds login, sessions, and protected routes using Remix's session helpers. Authentication enables per-user data and secure access control.
remix-dev.challenge: Add login so each user can manage only their own contacts using sessions.

remix-dev.title: Error Handling and Resilience
remix-dev.description: This layer covers error boundaries, status handling, and resilient UX. Graceful error handling is a core strength of the Remix model.
remix-dev.challenge: Add route-level error boundaries and friendly not-found pages to the app.

remix-dev.title: Advanced Data Patterns
remix-dev.description: This layer covers advanced fetching, caching, and optimistic patterns. These techniques make Remix apps feel fast and stay in sync.
remix-dev.challenge: Add inline editing with useFetcher and optimistic updates to the contacts list.

remix-dev.title: Styling and Performance
remix-dev.description: This layer covers building a responsive UI and optimizing performance. A clean, fast interface makes the application feel professional.
remix-dev.challenge: Apply a responsive Tailwind design and tune the app for strong Web Vitals.

remix-dev.title: Testing and Deployment
remix-dev.description: This layer covers testing the app and deploying it to production. Tests and an adapter-based deploy make the project a real, reliable product.
remix-dev.challenge: Add tests and deploy the Remix contacts app with a hosted database.
